---
title: 'Homework #8: Q15'
author: "Andrei Dominic Magno"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

Play the dice game described in exercise 4.3 multiple times. Estimate both the expected value and variance of your earnings.
For reference: Exercise 4.3 The following dice game costs $10 to play. Let W be your winnings from the game. If you roll 1, 2, or 3, you lose your money (W = –10). If you roll 4 or 5, you get your money back (W = 0). If you roll a 6, you receive $24 (W = 14).
Find the distribution of your winnings upper W.
Find the expected value of the game.

Use R to simulate and estimate the expected value and the variance for the winnings of this game.
Upload your code and output here.

```{r}
n <- 1000000
trials <- sample(1:6, size = n, replace = TRUE)

W <- ifelse(trials %in% c(1,2,3), -10,
     ifelse(trials %in% c(4,5), 0, 14))

expected_value <- mean(W)
variance <- var(W)

cat("Expected Value:", expected_value, "\n")
cat("Variance:", variance, "\n")
```
